summary: The universal content-addressed, recursive, shard-routable substrate (a database + graph) that mirrors CRM objects (leads, properties, routes) as Thingas, plus the KG projection persistence that records entities/edges/citations.

files.role: Core Thinga store: thingas / thinga_links / thinga_meta tables (SQLite).
Postgres-backed Thinga store (same shape) for the optional pg path.
Faceted Thinga layer (typed facets over the base Thinga).
Mirrors CRM objects (leads etc.) into Thingas.
Real-estate-specific Thinga shaping (config #1 of the substrate). No dedicated test file -- see tests note.
Real-estate facet definitions for faceted Thingas.
Parser registry for substrate payloads (parser node kind).
createKgPool() + kg_entities/kg_edges/kg_citations schema -- the KG projection persistence.
Mirrors property rows into the Thinga substrate.

routes.summary: Reads child Thingas of a lead.

tables.name: thinga_links
tables.role: Typed links between Thingas (from_id, link_kind, to_id).

docs.role: Real-estate operating model over the substrate.
Migration state from legacy tables to the substrate.

audit_findings.finding: Defines the one-datatype Thinga schema the substrate converges toward.
Tracks how much of the CRM is mirrored into Thingas vs still legacy-table only.
The substrate mirror exists, but most developers still think in legacy tables unless the projection explains which rows have Thingas.

next_actions.action: Add a dedicated real_estate_thinga.test.js so the real-estate shaping layer is independently protected.
Make 'which rows have Thingas' visible so the substrate mirror is not invisible to developers reading legacy tables.
